prompt:
Declaration (in markdown format):

# Distributed Ralph Loop

A pool of agents continuously consumes tasks from a shared backlog file in git.
Each agent claims a task, implements it, commits, and releases. The loop runs
until the backlog is empty, the budget is exhausted, or a human kills it.

## Worker pool
- 6 implementer agents on `sonnet-4.6`
- 1 reviewer agent on `opus-4.7`
- At most 2 agents on the same repo concurrently (avoid file-lock thrash)

## Backlog
- Source: `fix_plan.md` in `github.com/lambda-run/ops`
- Claim strategy: atomic branch lock with 10-minute TTL
- Release a claim on merge, abandon, or TTL expiry

## Coordination protocol
- Conflict resolution: last-write-wins after rebase
- Escalation: 3 consecutive rebase conflicts on a task → hand to reviewer pool
- Gossip: emit `claim`, `merge`, and `block` events to Slack `#ralph-bus`

## Loop conditions
- Continue while the backlog has open tasks
- Stop if any of: budget exceeds $50, human flips the kill switch, error rate > 30%

## Crash recovery
- On worker crash: release any claims it held
- Re-queue the orphaned task with `retry_count++`
- Max 3 retries, then park the task for human review

## Observability
- Log `claim`, `commit`, `fail` events to loki
- Metrics tracked: success_rate, p50 task seconds, claim contention
- Surface to human when error rate > 20% OR queue stuck > 30m

## Termination
Loop ends when the backlog is empty, the budget is exhausted, or the human signals done.

Output a flat bullet list manifest of every concrete element this declaration implies.
manifest:
- Distributed Ralph Loop
- Agent pool continuously consumes tasks from a shared backlog file in git
- Each agent claims a task
- Each agent implements a claimed task
- Each agent commits implemented work
- Each agent releases after committing
- Worker pool includes 6 implementer agents
- Implementer agents use `sonnet-4.6`
- Worker pool includes 1 reviewer agent
- Reviewer agent uses `opus-4.7`
- Maximum 2 agents may work on the same repo concurrently
- Backlog source is `fix_plan.md`
- Backlog repo is `github.com/lambda-run/ops`
- Claim strategy is atomic branch lock
- Claim lock TTL is 10 minutes
- Claims are released on merge
- Claims are released on abandon
- Claims are released on TTL expiry
- Conflict resolution uses last-write-wins after rebase
- 3 consecutive rebase conflicts on a task trigger escalation
- Escalated tasks are handed to the reviewer pool
- Gossip emits `claim` events
- Gossip emits `merge` events
- Gossip emits `block` events
- Gossip target is Slack channel `#ralph-bus`
- Loop continues while the backlog has open tasks
- Loop stops if budget exceeds $50
- Loop stops if a human flips the kill switch
- Loop stops if error rate is greater than 30%
- On worker crash, release any claims held by the crashed worker
- On worker crash, re-queue the orphaned task
- Re-queued orphaned tasks increment `retry_count`
- Maximum retries per task is 3
- Tasks exceeding 3 retries are parked for human review
- Log `claim` events to loki
- Log `commit` events to loki
- Log `fail` events to loki
- Track `success_rate` metric
- Track `p50 task seconds` metric
- Track `claim contention` metric
- Surface to human when error rate is greater than 20%
- Surface to human when queue is stuck for more than 30 minutes
- Loop ends when the backlog is empty
- Loop ends when the budget is exhausted
- Loop ends when the human signals done
